title: Chaetogastra repens
taxonomy: Chaetogastra repens is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically in the order Boraginales. It belongs to the family Boraginaceae and is further classified into the genus Chaetogastra. The species Chaetogastra repens is a type of flowering plant. It is native to the Americas and is known for its unique characteristics. Chaetogastra repens is a relatively rare species and is often studied by botanists for its distinct features.

intro: Chaetogastra repens, also known as False Paspalum, is a tropical plant species belonging to the grass family (Poaceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, skin conditions, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Chaetogastra repens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
